Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Canada Elections Act and to make consequential amendments to other Acts
Identification.ShortTitle: Canada Elections Act and to make consequential amendments to other Acts, An Act to amend the
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Canada Elections Act and to make consequential amendments to other Acts

Provision.Text: His Excellency the Governor General recommends to the House of Commons the appropriation of public revenue under the circumstances, in the manner and for the purposes set out in a measure entitled âAn Act to amend the Canada Elections Act and to make consequential amendments to other Actsâ.
This enactment amends the Canada Elections Act to

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: remove limitations on public education and information activities conducted by the Chief Electoral Officer;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: establish a Register of Future Electors in which Canadian citizens 14 to 17 years of age may consent to be included;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: authorize the Minister of Citizenship and Immigration to provide the Chief Electoral Officer with information about permanent residents and foreign nationals for the purpose of updating the Register of Electors;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: remove the prohibition on the Chief Electoral Officer authorizing the notice of confirmation of registration (commonly known as a âvoter information cardâ) as identification;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: replace, in the context of voter identification, the option of attestation for residence with an option of vouching for identity and residence;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: remove two limitations on voting by non-resident electors: the requirement that they have been residing outside Canada for less than five consecutive years, and the requirement that they intend to return to Canada to resume residence in the future; and

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: relocate the Commissioner of Canada Elections to within the Office of the Chief Electoral Officer, and provide that the Commissioner is to be appointed by the Chief Electoral Officer, after consultation with the Director of Public Prosecutions, for a non-renewable term of 10 years.
In addition, the enactment contains transitional provisions and makes consequential amendments to other Acts.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Subsection.Text: The definition Register of Electors in subsection 2(1) of the Canada Elections Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: Register of Electorsâmeans the Register of Electors established under paragraph 44(1)(a).â(Registre des Ã©lecteurs)
Register of Electorsâmeans the Register of Electors established under section 44.â(Registre des Ã©lecteurs)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: future electorâmeans a Canadian citizen who is 14 years of age or older but under 18 years of age.â(futur Ã©lecteur)
Register of Future Electorsâmeans the Register of Future Electors established under paragraph 44(1)(b).â(Registre des futurs Ã©lecteurs)

Section.Text: Section 11 of the Act is repealed.
Any of the following persons may vote in accordance with Part 11:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a Canadian Forces elector;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an elector who is an employee in the federal public administration or the public service of a province and who is posted outside Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a Canadian citizen who is employed by an international organization of which Canada is a member and to which Canada contributes and who is posted outside Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a person who has been absent from Canada for less than five consecutive years and who intends to return to Canada as a resident;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an incarcerated elector within the meaning of that Part; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any other elector in Canada who wishes to vote in accordance with that Part.

Section.Text: Subsection 15(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections 509.1(2) and (3), the Chief Electoral Officer shall rank as and have all the powers of a deputy head of a department, shall perform the duties of the office on a full-time basis and shall not hold any other office under Her Majesty or engage in any other employment.

The Chief Electoral Officer shall rank as and have all the powers of a deputy head of a department, shall perform the duties of the office on a full-time basis and shall not hold any other office under Her Majesty or engage in any other employment.

Section.Text: Section 17.1 of the Act is repealed.
The Chief Electoral Officer may implement public education and information programs to make the electoral process better known to students at the primary and secondary levels.
Subsections 18(1) to (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may implement public education and information programs to make the electoral process better known to the public, particularly to those persons and groups most likely to experience difficulties in exercising their democratic rights.

The Chief Electoral Officer may, using any media or other means that he or she considers appropriate, provide the public, both inside and outside Canada, with information relating to Canadaâs electoral process and the democratic rights to vote and to be a candidate.
The Chief Electoral Officer may establish programs to disseminate information outside Canada concerning how to vote under Part 11.
The Chief Electoral Officer shall ensure that any information on the following that is provided under subsections (1) to (1.2) in an advertising message is accessible to electors with disabilities:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: how to become a candidate;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: how an elector may have their name added to a list of electors and may have corrections made to information respecting them on the list;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: how an elector may vote under section 127 and the times, dates and locations for voting;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: how an elector may prove their identity and residence in order to vote, including the pieces of identification that they may use to that end; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the measures for assisting electors with a disability to access a polling station or advance polling station or to mark a ballot.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Communication with future electors with disabilities
Subsection.Text: If the Chief Electoral Officer provides, in an advertising message, any information under subsections (1) and (1.1) on how a future elector may have their name added to the Register of Future Electors and may have corrections made to information respecting them in that register, the Chief Electoral Officer shall ensure that the information is accessible to future electors with disabilities.

The Chief Electoral Officer may transmit or cause to be transmitted advertising messages, both inside and outside Canada, to inform electors about the exercise of their democratic rights. Such advertising messages shall only address

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: how to become a candidate;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: how an elector may have their name added to a list of electors and may have corrections made to information respecting the elector on the list;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: how an elector may vote under section 127 and the times, dates and locations for voting;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: how an elector may establish their identity and residence in order to vote, including the pieces of identification that they may use to that end; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the measures for assisting electors with a disability to access a polling station or advance polling station or to mark a ballot.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, subsection (1) does not prevent the Chief Electoral Officer from transmitting or causing to be transmitted advertising messages for any other purpose relating to his or her mandate.
The Chief Electoral Officer shall ensure that any information provided under subsection (1) is accessible to electors with disabilities.

Section.Text: The heading of Part 4 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Register of Electors and Register of Future Electors

Section.Text: The heading âMaintenance and Communication of Registerâ before section 44 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Section 44 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall maintain

Paragraph.Text: a register of persons who are qualified as electors, to be known as the Register of Electors; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a register of persons who are qualified as future electors, to be known as the Register of Future Electors.

Subsection.Text: The Register of Electors and the Register of Future Electors shall contain, for each elector or future elector who is included in it, as the case may be, their surname, given names, sex, date of birth, civic address, mailing address and any other information that is provided under subsections 49(2), 194(7), 195(7), 223(2), 233(2) and 251(3).

The Register of Electors and the Register of Future Electors shall also contain, for each elector or future elector, as the case may be, a unique, randomly generated identifier that is assigned by the Chief Electoral Officer.

Subject to subsection (5), the Register of Future Electors may continue to contain information about a former future elector after they have become an elector until they are included in the Register of Electors.
Inclusion in the Register of Electors and the Register of Future Electors is optional.
A future electorâs inclusion in the Register of Future Electors does not require the consent of the future electorâs parents or guardian or tutor.
The Chief Electoral Officer shall maintain a register of Canadians who are qualified as electors, to be known as the Register of Electors.

The Register of Electors shall contain, for each elector who is included in it, his or her surname, given names, sex, date of birth, civic address, mailing address and any other information that is provided under subsections 49(2), 194(7), 195(7), 223(2), 233(2) and 251(3).
The Register of Electors must also contain, for each elector, a unique, randomly generated identifier that is assigned by the Chief Electoral Officer.
Inclusion in the Register of Electors is at the option of the elector.

Section.Text: The heading before section 46 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 46(1) of the Act before subparagraph (b)(i) is replaced by the following:
The Register of Electors and the Register of Future Electors shall be updated from

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: information

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that electors or future electors, as the case may be, have given the Chief Electoral Officer, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: that is held by a federal department or body and that electors or future electors, as the case may be, have expressly authorized to be given to the Chief Electoral Officer; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: information that the Chief Electoral Officer considers reliable and necessary for updating the surname, given names, sex, date of birth, civic address and mailing address of electors and future electors included in the Register of Electors or the Register of Future Electors, as the case may be, and that

Subsection.Text: The Register of Electors shall be updated from

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: information

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that electors have given the Chief Electoral Officer, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: that is held by a federal department or body and that electors have expressly authorized to be given to the Chief Electoral Officer; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: information that the Chief Electoral Officer considers reliable and necessary for updating the surname, given names, sex, date of birth, civic address and mailing address of electors included in the Register of Electors and that

Subsection.Text: Subsection 46(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Source of information: Register of Future Electors
Subsection.Text: The Register of Electors shall be updated from information that the Chief Electoral Officer holds in the Register of Future Electors about future electors who become electors.

The Chief Electoral Officer may retain information collected under paragraph (1)(b), but not included in the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, for the purpose of correlating information subsequently collected with information already contained in the relevant register.

The Chief Electoral Officer may retain information collected under paragraph (1)(b), but not included in the Register of Electors, for the purpose of correlating information subsequently collected with information already contained in the Register of Electors.

Section.Text: Sections 46.1 and 46.2 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Information from Minister of Citizenship and Immigration
Section.Text: Despite subparagraph 46(1)(a)(ii), for the purpose of assisting the Chief Electoral Officer in updating the Register of Electors, including by deleting the name of a person who is not an elector, the Minister of Citizenship and Immigration may, at the request of the Chief Electoral Officer in writing, provide the Chief Electoral Officer with any of the following information, in relation to a person, that is contained in databases maintained by the Department of Citizenship and Immigration that relate to permanent residents and foreign nationals as those terms are defined in subsection 2(1) of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act and with the date when the information referred to in paragraph (d) was included or updated in those databases:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: their surname and given names;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: their sex;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: their date of birth; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: their addresses.

Section.Text: For the purpose of assisting the Chief Electoral Officer in updating the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, the Minister of National Revenue may, on a return of income referred to in subsection 150(1) of the Income Tax Act, request that an individual who is filing a return of income under paragraph 150(1)(d) of that Act indicate in the return whether they are a Canadian citizen.

Section.MarginalNote: Information in respect of deceased individuals
Section.Text: For the purpose of updating the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, the Minister of National Revenue shall, at the request of the Chief Electoral Officer, provide the name, date of birth and address of any individual to whom paragraph 150(1)(b) of the Income Tax Act applies if that individual has, in their last return of income filed under paragraph 150(1)(d) of that Act, authorized that Minister to provide their name, date of birth and address to the Chief Electoral Officer for the relevant register.

For the purpose of assisting the Chief Electoral Officer in updating the Register of Electors, the Minister of National Revenue may, on a return of income referred to in subsection 150(1) of the Income Tax Act, request that an individual who is filing a return of income under paragraph 150(1)(d) of that Act indicate in the return whether he or she is a Canadian citizen.

For the purpose of updating the Register of Electors, the Minister of National Revenue shall, at the request of the Chief Electoral Officer, provide the name, date of birth and address of any individual to whom paragraph 150(1)(b) of the Income Tax Act applies if that individual has, in his or her last return of income filed under paragraph 150(1)(d) of that Act, authorized that Minister to provide his or her name, date of birth and address to the Chief Electoral Officer for the Register of Electors.
Sections 47.1 to 51 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Between election periods, a returning officer shall perform any duties related to the updating of the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors that are requested by the Chief Electoral Officer.

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall, before including an elector in the Register of Electors or a future elector in the Register of Future Electors, send them the Chief Electoral Officerâs information relating to them and ask them if they wish to be included in the relevant register.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Obligation of elector or future elector
Subsection.Text: An elector or future elector who wishes to be included in the relevant register shall confirm, correct or complete the information, in writing, and give it to the Chief Electoral Officer along with a signed certification that they are qualified as an elector or future elector, as the case may be.
This section does not apply in respect of the inclusion of an elector or future elector that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: done at their request;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: done using information from lists of electors or future electors established under provincial law that contain information that the Chief Electoral Officer considers sufficient for the electorâs or future electorâs inclusion; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: done using information to update the Register of Electors under subsection 46(1.01).

Subsection.Text: Any person may at any time request the Chief Electoral Officer to include them in the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, by providing

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a signed certification that they are qualified as an elector or future elector, as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: their surname, given names, sex, date of birth, civic address and mailing address; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: satisfactory proof of identity.

Subsection.Text: In addition to the information referred to in subsection (1), the Chief Electoral Officer may invite the elector or future elector to give any other information that the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary to implement any agreements entered into under section 55, but the elector or future elector is not required to do so.

Section.Text: An elector or future elector may inform the Chief Electoral Officer of changes to the information in the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, as the case may be, relating to them, and the Chief Electoral Officer shall make the necessary corrections to the relevant register.
The Chief Electoral Officer may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: contact an elector or future elector to verify the Chief Electoral Officerâs information relating to them; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: request the elector or future elector to confirm, correct or complete the information within 60 days after receiving the request.

Section.Text: Between election periods, a returning officer shall perform any duties related to the updating of the Register of Electors that are requested by the Chief Electoral Officer.

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall, before including a new elector in the Register of Electors, send the elector the Chief Electoral Officerâs information relating to him or her and ask if he or she wishes to be included in the Register of Electors.

A new elector who wishes to be included in the Register of Electors shall confirm, correct or complete the information, in writing, and give it to the Chief Electoral Officer along with a signed certification that he or she is qualified as an elector under section 3.
This section does not apply in respect of the inclusion of a new elector

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at the electorâs request; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: based on lists of electors established under provincial law, if those lists contain the information that the Chief Electoral Officer considers sufficient for the inclusion of the elector.

Subsection.Text: Any person may at any time request the Chief Electoral Officer to include him or her in the Register of Electors, by providing

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a signed certification that he or she is qualified as an elector;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: his or her surname, given names, sex, date of birth, civic address and mailing address; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: satisfactory proof of identity.

Subsection.Text: In addition to the information referred to in subsection (1), the Chief Electoral Officer may invite the elector to give any other information that the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary to implement any agreements entered into under section 55, but the elector is not required to do so.

Section.Text: An elector may give the Chief Electoral Officer changes to the information in the Register of Electors relating to the elector, and the Chief Electoral Officer shall make the necessary corrections to the Register of Electors.
The Chief Electoral Officer may at any time

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: contact an elector to verify the Chief Electoral Officerâs information relating to him or her; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: request the elector to confirm, correct or complete the information within 60 days after receiving the request.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 52(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The Chief Electoral Officer shall delete from the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors the name of any person who
The Chief Electoral Officer shall delete from the Register of Electors the name of any person who

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is not an elector; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: requests in writing to have his or her name deleted;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: is under a court-ordered protective regime, including guardianship, tutorship or curatorship, and whose authorized representative under the regime requests in writing that the personâs name be deleted.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 52(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is not an elector or future elector, as the case may be, subject to subsection 44(4);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 52(1)(c) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: requests in writing to have their name deleted;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 52(1)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: is, by reason of mental incapacity, under a court-ordered protective regime, including guardianship, tutorship or curatorship, and whose authorized representative under the regime requests in writing that the personâs name be deleted; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: is a future elector with a mental incapacity whose parent requests in writing that the future electorâs name be deleted.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 52(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Chief Electoral Officer may delete from the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors the name of any person who fails to comply with a request referred to in paragraph 51(b) within the 60 days.
The Chief Electoral Officer may delete from the Register of Electors the name of any person who fails to comply with a request referred to in paragraph 51(b) within the 60 days.

Section.Text: Section 53 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 53(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: If a future elector so requests the Chief Electoral Officer in writing, information in the Register of Future Electors relating to them shall be used only for the purposes of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: updating the Register of Electors; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission of information in the course of public education and information programs implemented under subsection 18(1).

Section.Text: Section 54 of the Act is replaced by the following:
At the written request of an elector or future elector, the Chief Electoral Officer shall send them all the information in the Chief Electoral Officerâs possession relating to them.
At the written request of an elector, the Chief Electoral Officer shall send the elector all the information in the Chief Electoral Officerâs possession relating to him or her.
Subsection 55(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may enter into an agreement with any body responsible under provincial law for establishing a list of electors or future electors, governing the giving of information contained in the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, or the giving of information referred to in subsection 44(2) or (3) that the Chief Electoral Officer intends to include in either register, if that information is needed for establishing such a list.

The Chief Electoral Officer may enter into an agreement with any body responsible under provincial law for establishing a list of electors, governing the giving of information contained in the Register of Electors, or the giving of information referred to in subsection 44(2) or (2.1) that the Chief Electoral Officer intends to include in the Register of Electors, if that information is needed for establishing such a list.
Paragraphs 56(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly make a false or misleading statement, orally or in writing, relating to their qualification as an elector or future elector or relating to any other information referred to in section 49;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly make a false or misleading statement, orally or in writing, relating to another personâs qualification as an elector or future elector, to that other personâs surname, given names, sex, civic address or mailing address, or to the identifier assigned to that other person by the Chief Electoral Officer, for the purpose of having that other personâs name deleted from the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, as the case may be;

Section.Text: No person shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly make a false or misleading statement, orally or in writing, relating to their qualification as an elector or relating to any other information referred to in section 49;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly make a false or misleading statement, orally or in writing, relating to another personâs qualification as an elector, to the surname, given names, sex, civic address or mailing address of that person, or to the identifier assigned to that person by the Chief Electoral Officer, for the purpose of having that personâs name deleted from the Register of Electors;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: wilfully apply to have included in the Register of Electors the name of an animal or thing;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 56(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: wilfully apply to have included in the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors the name of an animal or thing;

Subsection.Text: Section 56 of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (e) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly use personal information that is obtained from the Register of Future Electors except as follows:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purposes of updating the Register of Electors,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purposes of the transmission of information in the course of public education and information programs implemented under subsection 18(1), or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: in accordance with the conditions included in an agreement made under section 55, in the case of information that is transmitted in accordance with the agreement; or

Section.Text: Paragraph 95(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: are incarcerated electors as defined in section 177;

Subsection.Text: Each returning officer shall, as soon as possible after the issue of a writ but not later than the 24th day before polling day, send a notice of confirmation of registration to every elector whose name appears on the preliminary list of electors, except electors who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: are referred to in paragraph 11(e);

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 143(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If the poll clerk determines that the electorâs name and address appear on the list of electors or that the elector is allowed to vote under section 146, 147, 148 or 149, then, subject to subsection (3), the elector shall provide the deputy returning officer and the poll clerk with the following proof of his or her identity and residence:

If the poll clerk determines that the electorâs name and address appear on the list of electors or that the elector is allowed to vote under section 146, 147, 148 or 149, then the elector shall provide the deputy returning officer and the poll clerk with the following proof of the electorâs identity and, subject to subsection (3), his or her residence:
Subsection 143(2.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Chief Electoral Officer may authorize types of identification for the purposes of paragraph (2)(b). For greater certainty, any document may be authorized, regardless of who issued it.

The Chief Electoral Officer may authorize types of identification for the purposes of paragraph (2)(b). For greater certainty, any documentâââother than a notice of confirmation of registration sent under section 95 or 102âââregardless of who issued the document, may be authorized.
Subsection 143(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

An elector may instead prove his or her identity and residence by taking an oath in writing in the prescribed formâââthe form including the statement that he or she knows the content of the written statement referred to in subsection 143.1(1)âââif he or she is accompanied by another elector whose name appears on the list of electors for the same polling division and who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: provides the deputy returning officer and the poll clerk with the piece or pieces of identification referred to in paragraph (2)(a) or (b), respectively; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: vouches for the elector on oath in writing in the prescribed form, the form including the statements that that other elector

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: knows the content of the written statement referred to in subsection 143.1(2),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: knows the elector being vouched for personally,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: knows that the elector being vouched for resides in the polling division,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: has not vouched for any other elector at the election, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: has not been vouched for by any other elector at the election.

Subsection.Text: An elector who proves his or her identity by providing two pieces of identification of a type authorized under subsection (2.1) that establish the electorâs name may instead prove his or her residence by taking an oath in writing in the prescribed formâââthe form including the statement that he or she has received the oral advice set out in subsection 143.1(1)âââif he or she is accompanied by another elector whose name appears on the list of electors for the same polling division who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: proves their own identity and residence to the deputy returning officer and poll clerk by providing the piece or pieces of identification referred to in paragraph (2)(a) or (b), respectively; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: attests to the electorâs residence on oath in writing in the prescribed form, the form including the statements that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: they have received the oral advice set out in subsection 143.1(2),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: they know the elector personally,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: they know that the elector resides in the polling division,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: they have not attested to the residence of another elector at the election, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: their own residence has not been attested to by another elector at the election.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 143(5) and (6) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Prohibitionâââvouching for more than one elector
Subsection.Text: No elector shall vouch for more than one elector at an election.
No elector who has been vouched for at an election shall vouch for another elector at that election.
No elector shall attest to the residence of more than one elector at an election.
No elector whose own residence has been attested to at an election shall attest to another electorâs residence at that election.

Section.Text: Section 143.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The prescribed form of the oath in writing by which an elector proves his or her identity and residence shall include a written statement of the qualifications for electors and the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who is found guilty of voting or attempting to vote at an election knowing that he or she is not qualified as an elector or on a person who contravenes subsection 549(3).

The prescribed form of the oath in writing by which another elector vouches for an elector shall include a written statement of the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who contravenes subsection 143(5) or (6) or 549(3).

If a person decides to prove his or her residence by taking an oath in writing in the prescribed form, the person who administers the oath shall, before doing so, orally advise the oath-taker of the qualifications for electors and the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who is found guilty of voting or attempting to vote at an election knowing that he or she is not qualified as an elector or who contravenes subsection 549(3).

If a person decides to attest to an electorâs residence by taking an oath in writing in the prescribed form, the person who administers the oath shall, before doing so, orally advise the oath-taker of the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who contravenes subsection 143(5) or (6) or 549(3).
The portion of subsection 161(1) of the French version of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
LâÃ©lecteur dont le nom ne figure pas dÃ©jÃ sur la liste Ã©lectorale peut, le jour du scrutin, sâinscrire en personneâsâil Ã©tablit son identitÃ© et sa rÃ©sidence :

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: soit en prÃ©sentant la piÃ¨ce visÃ©e Ã lâalinÃ©a 143(2)a) sur laquelle figure une adresse qui Ã©tablit sa rÃ©sidence ou les piÃ¨ces visÃ©es Ã lâalinÃ©a 143(2)b) dont au moins une porte une telle adresse;

Subsection.Text: An elector whose name is not on the list of electors may register in person on polling day if the elector

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: provides as proof of his or her identity and residence the piece or pieces of identification referred to in paragraph 143(2)(a) or (b), respectively, the piece or one of those pieces containing an address that proves his or her residence; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: proves his or her identity by providing two pieces of identification of a type authorized under subsection 143(2.1) that establish the electorâs name, proves his or her residence by taking an oath in writing in the prescribed formâââthe form including the statement that he or she has received the oral advice set out in subsection 161.1(1)âââand is accompanied by another elector whose name appears on the list of electors for the same polling division who

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: proves their own identity and residence by providing the piece or pieces of identification referred to in paragraph 143(2)(a) or (b), respectively, the piece or one of those pieces containing either an address that proves that other electorâs residence or an address that is consistent with information related to that other elector that appears on the list of electors, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: attests to the electorâs residence on oath in writing in the prescribed form, the form including the statements that

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: they have received the oral advice set out in subsection 161.1(2),

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: they know the elector personally,

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: they know that the elector resides in the polling division,

Clause.Label: (D)
Clause.Text: they have not attested to the residence of another elector at the election, and

Clause.Label: (E)
Clause.Text: their own residence has not been attested to by another elector at the election.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 161(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: proves his or her identity and residence by taking an oath in writing in the prescribed formâââthe form including the statement that he or she knows the content of the written statement referred to in subsection 161.1(1)âââand is accompanied by another elector whose name appears on the list of electors for the same polling division and who

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: provides the piece or pieces of identification referred to in paragraph 143(2)(a) or (b), respectively, the piece or one of those pieces containing either an address that proves that other electorâs residence or an address that is consistent with information related to that other elector that appears on the list of electors, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: vouches for the elector on oath in writing in the prescribed form, the form including the statements that that other elector

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: knows the content of the written statement referred to in subsection 161.1(2),

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: knows the elector being vouched for personally,

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: knows that the elector being vouched for resides in the polling division,

Clause.Label: (D)
Clause.Text: has not vouched for any other elector at the election, and

Clause.Label: (E)
Clause.Text: has not been vouched for by any other elector at the election.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 161(6) and (7) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Prohibitionâââvouching for more than one elector
Subsection.Text: No elector shall vouch for more than one elector at an election.
No elector who has been vouched for at an election shall vouch for another elector at that election.
No elector shall attest to the residence of more than one elector at an election.
No elector whose own residence has been attested to at an election shall attest to another electorâs residence at that election.

Section.Text: Section 161.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The prescribed form of the oath in writing by which an elector proves his or her identity and residence shall include a written statement of the qualifications for electors and the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who contravenes subsection 161(5.1) or 549(3).

The prescribed form of the oath in writing by which another elector vouches for an elector shall include a written statement of the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who contravenes subsection 161(6) or (7) or 549(3).

If a person decides to prove his or her residence by taking an oath in writing in the prescribed form, the person who administers the oath shall, before doing so, orally advise the oath-taker of the qualifications for electors and the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who contravenes subsection 161(5.1) or 549(3).

If a person decides to attest to an electorâs residence by taking an oath in writing in the prescribed form, the person who administers the oath shall, before doing so, orally advise the oath-taker of the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who contravenes subsection 161(6) or (7) or 549(3).
The portion of subsection 169(2) of the French version of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
Il ne peut toutefois Ãªtre inscrit que sâil Ã©tablit son identitÃ© et sa rÃ©sidence :

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: soit en prÃ©sentant la piÃ¨ce visÃ©e Ã lâalinÃ©a 143(2)a) sur laquelle figure une adresse qui Ã©tablit sa rÃ©sidence ou les piÃ¨ces visÃ©es Ã lâalinÃ©a 143(2)b) dont au moins une porte une telle adresse;

Subsection.Text: An elector shall not be registered unless he or she

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: provides as proof of his or her identity and residence the piece or pieces of identification referred to in paragraph 143(2)(a) or (b), respectively, the piece or one of those pieces containing an address that proves his or her residence; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: proves his or her identity by providing two pieces of identification of a type authorized under subsection 143(2.1) that establish the electorâs name, proves his or her residence by taking an oath in writing in the prescribed formâââthe form including the statement that he or she has received the oral advice set out in subsection 169.1(1)âââand is accompanied by another elector whose name appears on the list of electors for the same polling division who

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: proves their own identity and residence by providing the piece or pieces of identification referred to in paragraph 143(2)(a) or (b), respectively, the piece or one of those pieces containing either an address that proves that other electorâs residence or an address that is consistent with information related to that other elector that appears on the list of electors, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: attests to the electorâs residence on oath in writing in the prescribed form, the form including the statements that

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: they have received the oral advice set out in subsection 169.1(2),

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: they know the elector personally,

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: they know that the elector resides in the polling division,

Clause.Label: (D)
Clause.Text: they have not attested to the residence of another elector at the election, and

Clause.Label: (E)
Clause.Text: their own residence has not been attested to by another elector at the election.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 169(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: proves his or her identity and residence by taking an oath in writing in the prescribed formâââthe form including the statement that he or she knows the content of the written statement referred to in subsection 169.1(1)âââand is accompanied by another elector whose name appears on the list of electors for the same polling division and who

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: provides the piece or pieces of identification referred to in paragraph 143(2)(a) or (b), respectively, the piece or one of those pieces containing either an address that proves that other electorâs residence or an address that is consistent with information related to that other elector that appears on the list of electors, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: vouches for the elector on oath in writing in the prescribed form, the form including the statements that that other elector

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: knows the content of the written statement referred to in subsection 169.1(2),

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: knows the elector being vouched for personally,

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: knows that the elector being vouched for resides in the polling division,

Clause.Label: (D)
Clause.Text: has not vouched for any other elector at the election, and

Clause.Label: (E)
Clause.Text: has not been vouched for by any other elector at the election.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 169(5) and (6) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Prohibitionâââvouching for more than one elector
Subsection.Text: No elector shall vouch for more than one elector at an election.
No elector who has been vouched for at an election shall vouch for another elector at that election.
No elector shall attest to the residence of more than one elector at an election.
No elector whose residence has been attested to at an election shall attest to another electorâs residence at that election.

Section.Text: Section 169.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The prescribed form of the oath in writing by which an elector proves his or her identity and residence shall include a written statement of the qualifications for electors and the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who contravenes subsection 169(4.1) or 549(3).

The prescribed form of the oath in writing by which another elector vouches for an elector shall include a written statement of the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who contravenes subsection 169(5) or (6) or 549(3).

If a person decides to prove his or her residence by taking an oath in writing in the prescribed form, the person who administers the oath shall, before doing so, orally advise the oath-taker of the qualifications for electors and the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who contravenes subsection 169(4.1) or 549(3).

If a person decides to attest to an electorâs residence by taking an oath in writing in the prescribed form, the person who administers the oath shall, before doing so, orally advise the oath-taker of the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who contravenes subsection 169(5) or (6) or 549(3).

Section.Text: The heading of Division 3 of Part 11 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The definitions elector and register in section 220 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: electorâmeans an elector, other than a Canadian Forces elector, who resides outside Canada.â(Ã©lecteur)
registerâmeans the register referred to in section 222.â(registre)
electorâmeans an elector, other than a Canadian Forces elector, who resides temporarily outside Canada.â(Ã©lecteur)
registerâmeans the register referred to in subsection 222(1).â(re-gistre)

Section.Text: Section 222 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Chief Electoral Officer shall maintain a register of electors in which is entered the surname, given names, sex, date of birth, civic address, mailing address and electoral district of each elector who has filed an application for registration and special ballot and who, at any time before making the application, resided in Canada.

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall maintain a register of electors who are temporarily resident outside Canada in which is entered the name, date of birth, civic and mailing addresses, sex and electoral district of each elector who has filed an application for registration and special ballot and who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at any time before making the application, resided in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has been residing outside Canada for less than five consecutive years immediately before making the application; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: intends to return to Canada to resume residence in the future.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (1)(b) does not apply to an elector who is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: employed outside Canada in the federal public administration or the public service of a province;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: employed outside Canada by an international organization of which Canada is a member and to which Canada contributes;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a person who lives with an elector referred to in paragraph (a) or (b); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a person who lives with a member of the Canadian Forces or with a person referred to in paragraph 191(d).

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 223(1)(b) of the Act is repealed.
An application for registration and special ballot may be made by an elector. It shall be in the prescribed form and shall include

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if paragraph 222(1)(b) does not apply in respect of the elector, proof of the applicability of an exception set out in subsection 222(2);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the date the elector left Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the address of the electorâs last place of ordinary residence in Canada before he or she left Canada or the address of the place of ordinary residence in Canada of the spouse, the common-law partner or a relative of the elector, a relative of the electorâs spouse or common-law partner, a person in relation to whom the elector is a dependant or a person with whom the elector would live but for his or her residing temporarily outside Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the date on which the elector intends to resume residence in Canada;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 223(1)(d) to (f) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the address of the electorâs last place of ordinary residence in Canada before he or she left Canada;

Section.Text: Section 226 of the Act is amended by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (d), by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (e) and by repealing paragraph (f).
The Chief Electoral Officer shall delete from the register the name of an elector who

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: except for an elector to whom any of paragraphs 222(2)(a) to (d) applies, has resided outside Canada for five consecutive years or more.

Section.Text: Subsections 237.1(3.1) and (3.2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Prohibitionâââvouching for more than one elector
Subsection.Text: No elector shall vouch for more than one elector at an election.
No elector who has been vouched for at an election shall vouch for another elector at that election.
No elector shall attest to the residence of more than one elector at an election.
No elector whose own residence has been attested to at an election shall attest to another electorâs residence at that election.

Section.Text: Section 485 of the Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Offences under Part 4 (Register of Electors and Register of Future Electors)

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes paragraph 56(e) or (e.1) (unauthorized use of personal information recorded in Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors) is guilty of an offence.
Every person who contravenes any of paragraphs 56(a) to (d) (forbidden acts re Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors) is guilty of an offence.

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 31:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of the heading and section 485:

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes paragraph 56(e) (unauthorized use of personal information recorded in Register of Electors) is guilty of an offence.
Every person who contravenes any of paragraphs 56(a) to (d) (forbidden acts re Register of Electors) is guilty of an offence.
Paragraphs 489(2)(a) and (a.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 143(5) (vouching for more than one elector);

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 143(6) (vouchee acting as voucher);

Subsection.Text: Every person is guilty of an offence who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 143(5) (attesting to residence of more than one elector);

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 143(6) (attesting to residence when own residence attested to);

Paragraph.Label: (a.3)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 161(6) (attesting to residence of more than one elector);

Paragraph.Label: (a.4)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 161(7) (attesting to residence when own residence attested to);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 169(5) (attesting to residence of more than one elector); or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 169(6) (attesting to residence when own residence attested to).

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 489(2)(a.3) and (a.4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.3)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 161(6) (vouching for more than one elector);

Paragraph.Label: (a.4)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 161(7) (vouchee acting as voucher);

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 489(2)(d) and (e) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 169(5) (vouching for more than one elector); or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 169(6) (vouchee acting as voucher).

Section.Text: Paragraphs 491(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 237.1(3.1) (vouching for more than one elector);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 237.1(3.2) (vouchee acting as voucher); or

Subsection.Text: Every person is guilty of an offence who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 237.1(3.1) (attesting to residence of more than one elector);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 237.1(3.2) (attesting to residence when own residence attested to); or

Subsection.Text: Subsections 509(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

The Commissioner of Canada Elections shall be appointed by the Chief Electoral Officer, after consultation with the Director of Public Prosecutions, to hold office during good behaviour for a non-renewable term of 10 years and may be removed by the Chief Electoral Officer for cause.
The Commissioner shall be paid the remuneration that is fixed by the Chief Electoral Officer, after consultation with the Director of Public Prosecutions.

The Commissioner of Canada Elections shall be appointed by the Director of Public Prosecutions to hold office during good behaviour for a term of seven years and may be removed by the Director of Public Prosecutions for cause.
The Director of Public Prosecutions shall not consult the Chief Electoral Officer with respect to the appointment of the Commissioner.
Subsection 509(3) of the Act is amended by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (b) and by repealing paragraphs (d) and (e).
A person is not eligible to be appointed as Commissioner if the person is or has been

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the Chief Electoral Officer, a member of his or her staff or a person whose services have been engaged under subsection 20(1); or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an election officer referred to in any of paragraphs 22(1)(a) to (b).

Section.Text: Section 509.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Position within Office of Chief Electoral Officer

Subsection.Text: The position of Commissioner of Canada Elections is within the Office of the Chief Electoral Officer.

For the purposes of sections 11 to 13 of the Financial Administration Act, the Commissioner is the deputy head in relation to the portions of the federal public administration in the Office of the Chief Electoral Officer in which the employees referred to in section 509.3 occupy their positions.

For the purposes of the Public Service Employment Act, the Commissioner is the deputy head in relation to the portions of the federal public administration in the Office of the Chief Electoral Officer in which the employees referred to in section 509.3 occupy their positions.
The position of Commissioner of Canada Elections is within the Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions.

For the purposes of sections 11 to 13 of the Financial Administration Act, the Commissioner is the deputy head in relation to the portions of the federal public administration in the Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions in which the employees referred to in section 509.3 occupy their positions.

For the purposes of the Public Service Employment Act, the Commissioner is the deputy head in relation to the portions of the federal public administration in the Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions in which the employees referred to in section 509.3 occupy their positions.

Section.Text: Section 509.5 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Commissioner may authorize a person employed in the Office of the Chief Electoral Officer to assist the Commissioner in the exercise or performance of any of his or her powers, duties and functions arising from subsections 509.1(2) and (3) and in the exercise of his or her power under section 509.4, subject to the terms and conditions that the Commissioner sets.

The Commissioner may authorize a person employed in the Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions to assist him or her in the exercise or performance of any of his or her powers, duties and functions arising from subsections 509.1(2) and (3) and in the exercise of his or her power under section 509.4, subject to the terms and conditions that the Commissioner sets.
The portion of section 509.6 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Amounts to be paid out of C.R.F.
Section.Text: The following shall be paid out of unappropriated moneys forming part of the Consolidated Revenue Fund on the certificate of the Chief Electoral Officer:
The following shall be paid out of unappropriated moneys forming part of the Consolidated Revenue Fund on the certificate of the Director of Public Prosecutions:
Subsection 510(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner shall conduct the investigation independently of the Chief Electoral Officer.
The Commissioner is to conduct the investigation independently of the Director of Public Prosecutions.

Section.Text: Section 535 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Chief Electoral Officer shall, as soon as possible after a general election, make a report to the Speaker of the House of Commons that sets out any amendments that, in his or her opinion, are desirable for the better administration of this Act, and that also sets out, separately, any amendments that are set out in the Commissionerâs report under section 537.2.

The Chief Electoral Officer shall, as soon as possible after a general election, make a report to the Speaker of the House of Commons that sets out any amendments that, in his or her opinion, are desirable for the better administration of this Act.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 537:

The Commissioner shall, as soon as possible after the end of each year, publish, in the manner and form that he or she considers appropriate, a report on the activities of his or her office during that year. The Commissioner shall not include the details of any investigation.

The Commissioner shall, as soon as possible after a general election, make a report to the Chief Electoral Officer that sets out any amendments that, in the Commissionerâs opinion, are desirable for better compliance with, and the better enforcement of, this Act.

If, before the day on which section 28 comes into force, an application for registration and special ballot is made under section 223 of the Canada Elections Act, then Division 3 of Part 11 of that Act as it read immediately before that day applies for the purposes of the application.

Section.MarginalNote: Commissioner of Canada Electionsâââcontinuation of term
Section.Text: The person who holds the office of Commissioner of Canada Elections immediately before the day on which section 34 comes into force continues in office and is deemed to have been appointed under subsection 509(1) of the Canada Elections Act, as enacted by that section 34. However, his or her term of office begins on the date of his or her actual appointment.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, former portion means the portion of the federal public administration in the Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions in which, immediately before the day on which this section comes into force, the employees referred to in section 509.3 of the Canada Elections Act occupied their positions.

Any amount that was appropriated, for the fiscal year in which this section comes into force, for defraying the charges and expenses in respect of the former portion and that, on the day on which this section comes into force, is unexpended is deemed, on that day, to be an amount appropriated for defraying the charges and expenses of the Office of the Chief Electoral Officer for the purposes of the powers, duties and functions of the Commissioner of Canada Elections.

Any action, suit or other legal proceedings to which the Director of Public Prosecutions is a party relating to the former portion that is pending in any court immediately before the day on which this section comes into force may be continued by or against the Chief Electoral Officer in the same manner and to the same extent as it could have been continued by or against the Director of Public Prosecutions.

Nothing in this Act is to be construed as affecting the status of an employee who, immediately before the day on which this section comes into force, occupied a position in the former portion, except that the employee shall, beginning on that day, occupy their position in the Office of the Chief Electoral Officer.

Section.Text: Section 16.31 of the Access to Information Act is repealed.

Subject to section 541 of the Canada Elections Act, the Director of Public Prosecutions may refuse to disclose any record requested under this Act that contains information that was obtained or created by or on behalf of a person who conducts an investigation, examination or review in the performance of the functions of the Commissioner of Canada Elections under the Canada Elections Act.
Schedule IV to the Financial Administration Act is amended by striking out the following:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: The portion of the federal public administration in the Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions in which the employees referred to in section 509.3 of the Canada Elections Act occupy their positions
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Le secteur de lâadministration publique fÃ©dÃ©rale faisant partie du Bureau du directeur des poursuites pÃ©nales dans lequel les employÃ©s visÃ©s Ã lâarticle 509.3 de la Loi Ã©lectorale du Canada occupent un poste

Section.Text: Schedule IV to the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: The portion of the federal public administration in the Office of the Chief Electoral Officer in which the employees referred to in section 509.3 of the Canada Elections Act occupy their positions
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Le secteur de lâadministration publique fÃ©dÃ©rale faisant partie du bureau du directeur gÃ©nÃ©ral des Ã©lections dans lequel les employÃ©s visÃ©s Ã lâarticle 509.3 de la Loi Ã©lectorale du Canada occupent un poste

Section.Text: Subsection 3(2) of the Director of Public Prosecutions Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Director has the rank and status of a deputy head of a department.
Subject to subsections 509.1(2) and (3) of the Canada Elections Act, the Director has the rank and status of a deputy head of a department.

Section.Text: Subsection 6(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Under the supervision of the Director, a Deputy Director may also act for or on behalf of the Director in the exercise of any of the other powers or the performance of any of the other duties or functions that the Director is authorized to exercise or perform under this or any other Act of Parliament.

Under the supervision of the Director, a Deputy Director may also act for or on behalf of the Director in the exercise of any of the other powers or the performance of any of the other duties or functions that the Director is authorized to exercise or perform under this or any other Act of Parliament, except for the powers under subsection 509(1) of the Canada Elections Act.

Section.Text: Subsections 16(1) and (1.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Director shall, not later than June 30 of each year, provide a report to the Attorney General on the activities of the office of the Directorâââexcept in relation to matters referred to in subsection 3(8)âââin the immediately preceding fiscal year.
The Director shall, not later than June 30 of each year, provide a report to the Attorney General on the activities of the office of the Director in the immediately preceding fiscal year.

In addition, the report shall include a section, provided by the Commissioner of Canada Elections, on his or her activities under the Canada Elections Act in that fiscal year. The Commissioner shall not include the details of any investigation.

Section.Text: This Act comes into force on the day that, in the sixth month after the month in which it receives royal assent, has the same calendar number as the day on which it receives royal assentâââor, if that sixth month has no day with that number, the last day of that sixth monthâââunless, before then, the Chief Electoral Officer publishes a notice in the Canada Gazette that the necessary preparations for the bringing into operation of this Act have been made and that this Act may come into force accordingly, in which case it comes into force on the day on which the notice is published.
